parts.title: Short Pairs I
parts.text: The cat sat. A dog ran. The cat sat. A dog ran. The cat sat.

parts.title: Short Pairs II
parts.text: She reads books. He plays well. She reads books. He plays well.

parts.title: Short Pairs III
parts.text: They went today. The sun is warm. They went today. The sun is warm.

parts.title: Medium S1
parts.text: The cat sat on the mat. A big dog ran very fast down the road.

parts.title: Medium S2
parts.text: She likes to read good books. He can play the piano well each day.

parts.title: Medium S3
parts.text: They went to the park today. The sun is very bright and warm here.

parts.title: Medium S4
parts.text: Birds sing in the morning air. The wind blows through tall green trees.

parts.title: Combined I
parts.text: The cat sat on the mat and a big dog ran fast. She likes books.

parts.title: Combined II
parts.text: Birds sing each morning and the wind blows through the tall green trees.

parts.title: Sentence Mastery
parts.text: The cat sat on the mat. A big dog ran fast. She likes to read good books each day.

parts.text: (done) [ok] (done) [ok] (wait) [go] (done) [ok] (wait)

parts.title: Mixed I
parts.text: it's a beautiful day, isn't it? she said hello! wait - stop.

parts.title: Mixed II
parts.text: the price was $12.50, not $15.00. wait - who's there?

parts.title: Mixed III
parts.text: I can't believe it's already midnight. the result (if correct) will show (true).

parts.title: Symbol Mastery
parts.text: it's a beautiful day, isn't it? she said 'hello!' the price was $12.50, not $15.00.

parts.title: Nature I
parts.text: The sun rises every morning over distant mountains painting the sky in shades of gold and pink.

parts.title: Nature II
parts.text: Rivers flow through valleys carrying water to the sea while birds fly overhead in the clear blue sky.

parts.title: People I
parts.text: People gather to celebrate important events in their lives sharing food laughter and old warm memories.

parts.title: People II
parts.text: Children run and play in the warm afternoon sun while parents watch from the shade of tall trees.

parts.title: Technology I
parts.text: Technology changes how we live and work each passing decade bringing new tools and new ways of thinking.

parts.title: Technology II
parts.text: Computers connect people across the world sharing ideas and information at the speed of light every day.

parts.title: Learning I
parts.text: Knowledge grows through curiosity and persistent daily practice reading widely and asking good questions.

parts.title: Learning II
parts.text: The best students are those who never stop questioning and always seek to understand the world more deeply.

parts.title: Mixed Para
parts.text: The sun rises over mountains while people gather below. Technology connects us all and knowledge grows each day.

parts.title: Para Mastery
parts.text: Technology changes how we live each decade. Computers connect people sharing ideas while knowledge grows through curiosity and practice.

parts.title: Parentheses
parts.text: the result (if correct) will show (true) otherwise (false) check again (now)

parts.title: Colon Lists
parts.text: the items are: first, second, third. the colors are: red, green, blue.

parts.title: Full Mixed I
parts.text: it's a beautiful day, isn't it? the price was $12.50 (not $15.00) - check again.

parts.title: Full Mixed II
parts.text: I can't believe it's midnight already! wait - who's there? the result (if true) shows now.

parts.title: Punct Mastery
parts.text: it's a beautiful day, isn't it? she said 'hello!' the price was $12.50, not $15.00 - I can't believe it.

title: Long Paragraphs
desc: Extended passages for endurance and consistency

parts.title: Science I
parts.text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you. We are all connected biologically to the earth and atomically to the universe.

parts.title: Science II
parts.text: Not only are we in the universe the universe is in us. Every atom in your body came from a star that exploded long ago.

parts.title: Philosophy I
parts.text: The unexamined life is not worth living. Wisdom begins in wonder and ends in understanding the world around you deeply.

parts.title: Philosophy II
parts.text: In the middle of every difficulty lies opportunity. Imagination is more important than knowledge for it is the preview of life.

parts.title: History I
parts.text: Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it. History is the story of humanity written across thousands of years.

parts.title: History II
parts.text: Great civilizations rise and fall leaving behind art language and ideas that shape the world for all who come after them.

parts.title: Literature I
parts.text: It was the best of times it was the worst of times it was the age of wisdom it was the age of foolishness.

parts.title: Literature II
parts.text: Call me Ishmael. Some years ago never mind how long precisely having little money I thought I would sail about a little.

parts.title: Mixed Long
parts.text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense. In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity. The unexamined life is not worth living.

parts.title: Long Mastery
parts.text: Not only are we in the universe the universe is in us. Every atom in your body came from a star. Wisdom begins in wonder and ends in understanding.

title: Expert Passages
desc: Literary and academic paragraphs at expert level

parts.title: Cosmos I
parts.text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you. We are all connected to each other biologically to the earth chemically.

parts.title: Cosmos II
parts.text: Not only are we in the universe the universe is in us. I do not know of any deeper spiritual feeling than knowing you are made of star stuff.

parts.title: Psychology I
parts.text: The greatest discovery of my generation is that human beings can alter their lives by altering their attitudes of mind and their beliefs.

parts.title: Psychology II
parts.text: Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response. In our response lies our growth.

parts.title: Economics I
parts.text: In the long run we are all dead. Economists set themselves too easy a task if in tempestuous seasons they can only tell us that when the storm is long past the ocean is flat again.

parts.title: Physics I
parts.text: Imagination is more important than knowledge. Knowledge is limited whereas imagination encircles the world and points to all there will ever be.

parts.title: Physics II
parts.text: If you cannot explain it simply you do not understand it well enough. Everything should be made as simple as possible but not simpler.

parts.title: Biology I
parts.text: Nothing in biology makes sense except in the light of evolution. Life is the most extraordinary phenomenon in the known universe.

parts.title: Mixed Expert
parts.text: Between stimulus and response there is a space. Not only are we in the universe the universe is in us. Imagination encircles the world.

parts.title: Expert Mastery
parts.text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you. Between stimulus and response is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response.
